messages.content: today was just a lot. it's the anniversary of my mom passing and i didn't really tell anyone i was struggling with it

That kind of day carries a particular weight — holding something that big quietly, without anyone around you knowing.

What made you decide to keep it to yourself today?
thanks for just sitting with me on it. i don't usually let myself actually cry but i think i needed to

Something about finally letting that out — even here, even now — matters. Grief doesn't keep a tidy schedule.

How are you feeling right now, in this moment?
i'm still pretty heavy ngl, it hasn't really lifted. but i've got an early start and i don't wanna keep going tonight, i think i just need to stop

That sounds like the right call — knowing when to set it down for the night is its own kind of wisdom.

I hope you get some rest. She sounds like someone worth carrying, even on the hard days.
yeah. gonna go lie down in the dark for a while. night
Good night. Take care of yourself.
